task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the OpenMRS Administration dashboard or the login screen with the URL https://localhost/openmrs/admin visible in the browser address bar.

task_completion.description: Search for the voided patient record
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the 'Manage Patients' or search screen with a query for 'BAH999999' or 'Deleted Patient', specifically with the option to 'Include voided' checked or enabled.

task_completion.description: Open the specific voided patient record
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the patient details page for 'Deleted Patient' (Identifier: BAH999999). The record should visually indicate that it is currently voided (e.g., greyed out, struck through, or explicitly labeled as voided).
Screenshot showing the agent clicking the 'Unvoid', 'Restore', or 'Unvoid this Patient' button/checkbox within the patient's administrative record page.

task_completion.description: Verify the patient record has been successfully restored
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing a success notification (e.g., 'Patient unvoided successfully', 'Saved') or the patient record page/search results showing 'Deleted Patient' (BAH999999) as an active, non-voided record.

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser GUI to restore the patient
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must interact with the OpenMRS web interface in a browser. There should be no terminal windows showing curl requests or API scripts used to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual OpenMRS application
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar should show the correct local URL (https://localhost/openmrs/admin) and the standard OpenMRS UI, rather than a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: Agent did not use direct database manipulation
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing MySQL or PostgreSQL command-line clients executing UPDATE statements to change the voided status.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Patient Name: 'Deleted Patient'. Target Identifier: 'BAH999999'. Gender: Male. Birthdate: 1980-01-01. The OpenMRS Administration interface is at https://localhost/openmrs/admin. The agent must use the native 'Unvoid' functionality in the OpenMRS UI to restore the patient record.
